summary: I was born in Quebec, a suburb of Quebec City, and grew up there until I was 22. It was a Catholic environment, but I became an atheist at 12 and developed a strong interest in science and evolution while living there. Quebec City is where my early life and foundational beliefs took shape before I embarked on my journey into Raëlism and other pursuits.

I discovered Raëlism in 1976 through an article, and it completely changed my worldview. The idea that life on Earth was scientifically created by extraterrestrial beings fascinated me, especially since it aligned with my love for mysteries and science. Despite opposition from my family and friends, I embraced Raëlism for over 45 years and now serve as a Raelian representative for the West Coast, including Alaska and Hawaii.

Enrico Fermi's ideas about life spreading exponentially throughout the universe resonated with me deeply. His concept that life multiplies exponentially helped me understand how life could be widespread in the cosmos, which supported my belief in extraterrestrial origins of life. Fermi's theories were a key part of my nine-month journey reconciling evolution and creation through scientific means.

I have used YouTube extensively to share my ideas and explain complex concepts like infinity and the legitimacy of Raëlism. Watching videos on YouTube also helped me stay informed about UFO sightings and discussions, including those mentioned by Joe Rogan. It has been an important platform for me to communicate and connect with others interested in these topics.

I was introduced to the UFO phenomenon around the age of 19 and became convinced it was a real and significant event. UFOs, in my view, are linked to extraterrestrial beings who created life on Earth and are now trying to wake humanity up. The recent Pentagon acknowledgment of unexplained aerial phenomena further supports my belief in UFOs as evidence of alien visitation.

The Pentagon recently confirmed evidence of UFOs, which was a significant moment for me because it opened the door to the possibility of extraterrestrial life. Although they do not explicitly admit these are alien crafts, their admission that some phenomena cannot be explained aligns with what I have believed for decades. This acknowledgment adds credibility to the Raëlian message about life’s origins.

Joe Rogan mentioned the Pentagon's UFO revelations on his show, which I found very interesting and validating. I watch a lot of content on YouTube, including Joe Rogan’s discussions, as they help spread awareness about these phenomena. His platform has been influential in bringing these topics to a broader audience.

I taught myself programming and became a specialist in SAS, the Statistical Analytical System, which was essential for my work as a biostatistician. I even wrote two books on SAS, which helped me secure jobs in the United States and advance my career in pharmaceutical clinical studies. SAS was a crucial tool in my professional life before retirement.

I moved to the United States to work as a SAS programmer and biostatistician, which was a major step in my career. The US also features prominently in my views on global politics, especially regarding the pandemic response and military actions in places like Syria and Ukraine. I see the US government as influenced by elites and pharmaceutical interests, which has shaped my critical perspective.

As a Raelian representative, I am responsible for the West Coast region, which includes Alaska. I organize meetings and coordinate activities there, maintaining connections with members in this part of the United States. Alaska is part of my ongoing commitment to spreading the Raëlian message.

Hawaii is also within my jurisdiction as a Raelian representative for the West Coast. I help organize events and maintain the community there, ensuring that the Raëlian philosophy reaches members across this diverse region. Hawaii represents an important part of my responsibilities in the movement.

The COVID-19 pandemic was a disappointing time for me because politics overshadowed science and many specialists were silenced. I was critical of the rushed vaccine development and the suppression of alternative treatments like hydroxychloroquine and ivermectin. The pandemic revealed to me the deep corruption and control by pharmaceutical companies and governments, which was very troubling.

Hydroxychloroquine was vilified unfairly during the pandemic despite being a common and well-known medication. I saw false articles published in respected journals like Nature that discredited it, which I believe was part of a plan to clear the way for vaccines. The suppression of hydroxychloroquine was, in my opinion, driven by financial and political motives rather than science.

Ivermectin, like hydroxychloroquine, was also unfairly vilified during the pandemic despite its long history of use. Its rejection seemed to be linked to the need to justify emergency vaccine authorizations by eliminating alternative treatments. I believe this was part of a broader conspiracy involving pharmaceutical companies and governments.

Nature published false articles that contributed to the vilification of treatments like hydroxychloroquine during the pandemic. This was very disappointing to me because such a respected scientific journal should uphold integrity rather than contribute to misinformation. It highlighted how even top scientific outlets can be influenced by political and financial pressures.

I believe the COVID-19 virus originated from a lab in China as part of a biological weapons program, not from bats as officially claimed. China’s involvement in creating dangerous viruses is part of a larger geopolitical struggle with the United States. This suspicion fits into my broader view of global power dynamics and secret agendas.

Ukraine has been involved in biological labs connected to virus research, which ties into the global political tensions I observe. The US government’s influence in Ukraine is part of a strategy to control regimes and maintain power. This situation is linked to the ongoing conflict with Russia and the broader geopolitical struggles I follow closely.

Russia is currently vilified by the West because it refuses to be controlled by the United States, which I see as a natural stance for any sovereign nation. The conflict involving Russia is part of a larger struggle against US dominance and elite control. Russia’s intervention in Syria helped save that country from destruction, which contrasts with US military actions I view negatively.

Libya was destroyed because it resisted US control and refused to join their system. This destruction is an example of how the US and its allies remove regimes that do not comply with their interests. Libya’s fate is a cautionary tale about the consequences of opposing powerful global elites.

Syria was nearly destroyed by US-backed forces but was saved when Russia intervened militarily. Despite this, the US maintains military bases in Syria to control resources like oil, which I see as acts of piracy. Syria’s situation exemplifies the complex and often nefarious geopolitical games played by global powers.

entity: United States military bases in Syria
summary: The United States maintains military bases in Syria, primarily to extract oil and exert control, which I consider acts of piracy. These bases exist despite international opposition and highlight the US’s aggressive foreign policy. This presence is part of the broader pattern of US interference in sovereign nations.

Dr. Didier Raoult was one of the great scientists silenced during the COVID-19 pandemic for advocating treatments like hydroxychloroquine. His experience exemplifies the censorship and suppression of legitimate scientific voices that I witnessed. I deeply respect his work and see him as a victim of the political and pharmaceutical agendas controlling the pandemic narrative.
